Luis Mejía.bio: Panama's number one goalkeeper playing for Leganes in La Liga, making him one of the highest-profile Panamanian players in European football. With 30 caps, he brings top-flight experience.
Luis Mejía.playingStyle: Tall, commanding goalkeeper with strong shot-stopping, good positioning, and the ability to handle La Liga-level pressure
Luis Mejía.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper and one of the team's most valuable assets due to his La Liga pedigree
Luis Mejía.notableFact: One of the few Panamanian players to play regularly in a top-five European league

Michael Murillo.bio: Experienced right-back at Anderlecht in Belgium's top flight with 55 caps and 2 goals. One of Panama's most well-traveled players, having played in MLS and multiple European leagues.
Michael Murillo.playingStyle: Quick, athletic right-back who combines defensive strength with explosive attacking runs and dangerous crosses
Michael Murillo.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back whose speed and European experience are vital to Panama's defensive and attacking plans
Michael Murillo.notableFact: Represented Panama at the 2018 World Cup in Russia, making him one of the few players with previous World Cup experience

Harold Cummings.bio: Veteran center-back with 70 caps who is one of the most experienced players in the Panamanian squad. Currently playing in Guatemala, he brings years of international experience and leadership.
Harold Cummings.playingStyle: Physical, experienced center-back who relies on positioning, aerial ability, and defensive organization
Harold Cummings.worldCup2026Role: Senior defender providing leadership, organization, and experience in the heart of the defense
Harold Cummings.notableFact: A veteran of Panama's historic first World Cup appearance in 2018 in Russia

Fidel Escobar.bio: Experienced center-back with 60 caps who is one of the pillars of Panama's defense. Playing domestically in the Liga Panamena, he has been a loyal servant of the national team.
Fidel Escobar.playingStyle: Tough, physical defender who excels in one-on-one situations and is a strong presence in aerial duels
Fidel Escobar.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and defensive cornerstone of the team
Fidel Escobar.notableFact: Was part of the Panama squad at the 2018 World Cup and has continued to be a key player through the 2026 qualifying cycle

Erick Davis.bio: Center-back with European experience at Slovan Bratislava in Slovakia's top division. With 30 caps, he brings a European defensive education to Panama's backline.
Erick Davis.playingStyle: Athletic, mobile defender who covers ground well and is comfortable stepping out of the defensive line to intercept
Erick Davis.worldCup2026Role: Defensive option providing depth and European-level quality at center-back
Erick Davis.notableFact: One of two Davis players in the Panama squad, with Eric Davis at left-back being a separate player

Adalberto Carrasquilla.bio: Midfield maestro playing for Houston Dynamo in MLS with 40 caps and 3 goals. Widely considered the most talented midfielder in Panamanian football, he is the creative engine of the team.
Adalberto Carrasquilla.playingStyle: Technical, creative midfielder with excellent ball control, incisive passing, and the ability to see passes others cannot
Adalberto Carrasquilla.worldCup2026Role: Starting central midfielder and chief playmaker who controls Panama's tempo and creates chances
Adalberto Carrasquilla.notableFact: Won the CONCACAF Gold Cup Best Young Player award, establishing himself as one of the region's top midfield talents

César Yanis.bio: Exciting young winger at Orlando City in MLS with 20 caps and 3 goals. One of Panama's most promising attackers whose pace and directness trouble any defense.
César Yanis.playingStyle: Fast, direct winger who takes on defenders with confidence, delivers dangerous crosses, and cuts inside to shoot
César Yanis.worldCup2026Role: Starting or rotation winger whose speed and creativity are central to Panama's attacking threat
César Yanis.notableFact: Has established himself as one of the most exciting Panamanian attackers of the new generation

Édgar Bárcenas.bio: Panama's most experienced attacker with 60 caps and 8 goals, playing for Girona in La Liga. His European pedigree makes him one of the most valuable players in the squad.
Édgar Bárcenas.playingStyle: Versatile, experienced winger who can play on either flank, with good technical ability and a keen eye for goal
Édgar Bárcenas.worldCup2026Role: Key attacker whose La Liga experience and goal-scoring ability make him indispensable to Panama's World Cup campaign
Édgar Bárcenas.notableFact: Has been a consistent goal threat for Panama over many years and is one of the team's all-time top scorers among active players

Freddy Góndola.bio: Experienced left winger with 35 caps and 5 goals who has traveled the football world playing in various leagues. His pace and directness provide a different dimension to Panama's attack.
Freddy Góndola.playingStyle: Pacy, direct winger who uses speed to stretch defenses and create space for teammates
Freddy Góndola.worldCup2026Role: Experienced wide option providing attacking depth and the ability to stretch defenses
Freddy Góndola.notableFact: Has played in multiple countries across the Americas, bringing diverse football experience to the national team

Yoel Bárcenas.bio: Skilled right winger at Leganes in La Liga with 30 caps and 3 goals. Part of the Panamanian contingent in Spanish football, he adds European quality to the attacking options.
Yoel Bárcenas.playingStyle: Technically gifted winger with good close control, creative dribbling, and the ability to provide assists and goals
Yoel Bárcenas.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking player on the right wing, bringing La Liga quality and creativity to Panama's attack
Yoel Bárcenas.notableFact: Plays alongside goalkeeper Luis Mejia at Leganes, forming a Panamanian duo in La Liga

Cecilio Waterman.bio: Experienced striker with 25 caps and 8 goals who leads the line with physicality and predatory instincts. Playing domestically with Tauro, he is a proven goalscorer in Panamanian football.
Cecilio Waterman.playingStyle: Physical, target-man striker who holds the ball up well, wins aerial duels, and finishes clinically inside the box
Cecilio Waterman.worldCup2026Role: Experienced striking option providing a physical presence and aerial threat in attack
Cecilio Waterman.notableFact: Has been one of the most prolific scorers in the Liga Panamena across multiple seasons

Orlando Mosquera.bio: Goalkeeper playing for Millonarios in Colombia's top division with 15 caps for Panama. His experience in competitive South American football adds depth to the goalkeeping department.
Orlando Mosquera.playingStyle: Athletic goalkeeper with good reflexes, strong handling, and experience in competitive South American football
Orlando Mosquera.worldCup2026Role: Second-choice goalkeeper providing quality backup and competition
Orlando Mosquera.notableFact: His experience playing for Millonarios in Colombia's Liga gives him exposure to a higher competitive level than most domestic-based keepers

Eric Davis.bio: Veteran left-back with 65 caps and 1 goal, one of the most experienced players in the entire Panamanian squad. Playing in Slovakia's top division, he has been a loyal servant of the national team.
Eric Davis.playingStyle: Experienced, dependable left-back who provides width, defensive solidity, and leadership from the back
Eric Davis.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back and one of the most experienced players on the team, providing leadership and stability
Eric Davis.notableFact: Was part of Panama's historic 2018 World Cup squad and remains a crucial figure in the team's defensive setup

José Fajardo.bio: Clinical striker at Cincinnati FC in MLS with 25 caps and 8 goals. His goalscoring record for both club and country makes him one of Panama's most dangerous attackers.
José Fajardo.playingStyle: Sharp, instinctive striker with excellent finishing, good movement in the box, and the ability to score from tight angles
José Fajardo.worldCup2026Role: Starting striker or key rotation option whose goal-scoring instincts are vital to Panama's chances
José Fajardo.notableFact: Has adapted well to MLS football and become one of the most reliable Panamanian goalscorers in the league

Aníbal Godoy.bio: Panama's most capped active player with an incredible 130 caps and 3 goals. The veteran defensive midfielder from Nashville SC is the anchor and heartbeat of the national team.
Aníbal Godoy.playingStyle: Experienced, cerebral defensive midfielder who reads the game superbly, breaks up attacks, and distributes the ball with intelligence
Aníbal Godoy.worldCup2026Role: Veteran midfield anchor and spiritual leader whose 130 caps of experience are irreplaceable
Aníbal Godoy.notableFact: With 130 caps, he is one of the most capped CONCACAF players of his generation and a true legend of Panamanian football

José Luis Rodríguez.bio: Experienced central midfielder at Club America in Liga MX with 45 caps and 2 goals. Playing in one of the strongest leagues in CONCACAF gives him high-level competitive experience.
José Luis Rodríguez.playingStyle: Combative, energetic midfielder who excels at winning the ball and distributing it to more creative players
José Luis Rodríguez.worldCup2026Role: Key midfield option providing energy, defensive work, and the competitive edge from Liga MX
José Luis Rodríguez.notableFact: Playing for Club America, one of the biggest clubs in the Americas, gives him regular experience in high-pressure matches

José Murillo.bio: Young center-back at Minnesota United in MLS who is part of Panama's defensive future. At 26 with 10 caps, he is still developing but has shown strong potential.
José Murillo.playingStyle: Athletic, modern center-back with good pace, strong marking ability, and comfort on the ball
José Murillo.worldCup2026Role: Young defensive option providing depth and fresh legs at center-back
José Murillo.notableFact: Plays alongside New Zealand's Michael Boxall at Minnesota United, creating a World Cup connection at club level

José Guerra.bio: Young goalkeeper from Tauro in the Liga Panamena with 5 caps. He rounds out Panama's goalkeeping contingent and represents the future of Panamanian goalkeeping.
José Guerra.playingStyle: Young, agile goalkeeper with good reflexes and developing command of his area
José Guerra.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper gaining World Cup experience for future tournaments
José Guerra.notableFact: One of the youngest goalkeepers in any World Cup squad, his inclusion shows Panama's investment in developing young talent

Ismael Díaz.bio: Left winger with experience in Spanish football at AD Alcorcon in La Liga 2. With 20 caps and 2 goals, he brings European experience and technical quality to Panama's flanks.
Ismael Díaz.playingStyle: Tricky, skillful winger with good close control, creative dribbling, and the ability to create chances in tight spaces
Ismael Díaz.worldCup2026Role: Attacking rotation option who can change the game with his skill and creativity from the left side
Ismael Díaz.notableFact: Has been part of the growing Panamanian presence in Spanish football across multiple divisions

Jovani Welch.bio: Experienced center-back with 30 caps who plays domestically with Sporting San Jose. A reliable defender who has been a steady presence in the national team across multiple qualifying campaigns.
Jovani Welch.playingStyle: Solid, physical center-back who competes hard in duels and provides experienced defensive cover
Jovani Welch.worldCup2026Role: Backup center-back providing defensive depth and experience from the bench
Jovani Welch.notableFact: Has been a consistent performer for Panama across multiple Gold Cup and World Cup qualifying campaigns

Jair Catuy.bio: Young central midfielder from Alianza in the Liga Panamena, one of the youngest players in Panama's squad. With 5 caps, he represents the emerging generation of Panamanian midfield talent.
Jair Catuy.playingStyle: Energetic, young midfielder with good ball skills, composure on the ball, and growing tactical awareness
Jair Catuy.worldCup2026Role: Young midfield option providing fresh energy and development opportunity in the World Cup squad
Jair Catuy.notableFact: One of the youngest members of the squad, his inclusion shows Panama's commitment to building for the future

Rolando Blackburn.bio: Veteran striker with 30 caps and 5 goals, playing for Lechia Gdansk in Poland's Ekstraklasa. His experience in European football adds depth and variety to Panama's forward line.
Rolando Blackburn.playingStyle: Strong, physical striker who combines hold-up play with aerial ability and a willingness to run the channels
Rolando Blackburn.worldCup2026Role: Experienced forward option who can provide a physical presence and aerial threat off the bench
Rolando Blackburn.notableFact: Has built a career across multiple European countries, becoming one of Panama's most well-traveled players

Abdiel Ayarza.bio: Central midfielder with 25 caps and 2 goals who plays for Tauro in the Liga Panamena. A reliable, hard-working presence in the midfield who has been a steady contributor to the national team.
Abdiel Ayarza.playingStyle: Industrious, box-to-box midfielder who covers ground effectively and contributes in both defensive and attacking phases
Abdiel Ayarza.worldCup2026Role: Midfield depth option providing energy and defensive cover when the team needs fresh legs
Abdiel Ayarza.notableFact: A Tauro stalwart who has been one of the most consistent performers in Panamanian domestic football

Eduardo Anderson.bio: Young right-back from Sporting San Jose in the Liga Panamena with 8 caps. One of the youngest defenders in the squad, he is gaining international experience rapidly.
Eduardo Anderson.playingStyle: Young, developing right-back who is quick, eager to learn, and improving his defensive and attacking contributions
Eduardo Anderson.worldCup2026Role: Young backup right-back gaining experience and providing squad depth in defensive positions
Eduardo Anderson.notableFact: One of the youngest defenders to be called up to the Panamanian national team in recent years

Ivan Anderson.bio: Right-back at Real Oviedo in Spain's La Liga 2 with 15 caps. His experience in Spanish football makes him a valuable defensive option with European tactical awareness.
Ivan Anderson.playingStyle: Tactically astute right-back who has absorbed Spanish defensive principles and combines positional play with overlapping runs
Ivan Anderson.worldCup2026Role: Versatile right-back option who can compete for the starting position or provide quality backup
Ivan Anderson.notableFact: Part of the Panamanian contingent in Spanish football, gaining experience in one of the world's most tactically demanding football cultures

Jezreel Corrales.bio: Young left-back from Tauro in the Liga Panamena with 5 caps. A natural left-footer who provides backup at left-back and represents Panama's defensive future.
Jezreel Corrales.playingStyle: Attacking left-back with good crossing ability, pace, and a natural left foot for delivery
Jezreel Corrales.worldCup2026Role: Backup left-back providing youth and a left-footed option behind Eric Davis
Jezreel Corrales.notableFact: One of the youngest players in the squad, he has the opportunity to develop into Panama's long-term left-back solution
